revision.text:
[[ja:羽黒神社 (珠洲市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Haguro Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Haguro-jinja (Suzu), Torii.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Suzu, Ishikawa|en|Suzu, Ishikawa|qid=Q846345|lt=Suzu|1=Suzu, Ishikawa}} Shoin-machi Koji 16-102 | ISO = JP-11 | LatitudeDegrees = 37 | LatitudeMinutes = 26 | LatitudeSeconds = 57 | LongitudeDegrees = 137 | LongitudeMinutes = 17 | LongitudeSeconds = 32 | Deities = {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} | Rank = Village Shrine (村社) | consecration_year = During the {{ill|Bunmei|en|Bunmei|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei era|1=Bunmei}} | MainBuilding = | AnnualFestival = Spring Festival (April 19)
Autumn Festival (September 19) | Map = Japan Ishikawa }} '''Haguro Shrine''' (羽黒神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suzu, Ishikawa|en|Suzu, Ishikawa|qid=Q846345|lt=Suzu|1=Suzu, Ishikawa}}, {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. It serves as the guardian deity (産土神) for the entire Koji area of Shoin-machi and acts as the central shrine for ten shrines across various nearby districts{{Cite web|url=https://hagurojinja.amebaownd.com/pages/2318354/page_201810111436 |title=Haguro Shrine History |publisher=Haguro Shrine |accessdate=2022-01-01}}. == Enshrined Deities == === Primary deity === * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} (God of Agriculture and Food) === Additional Enshrined Deities === * {{ill|Kikurihime|ja|菊理姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikurihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}} == History == The shrine has been revered for centuries under names like "Haguro-no-miya" and "Ideha-no-miya Haguro Daigongen." According to the {{ill|Bunsei Shrine Register|ja|文政社号帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunsei Shrine Register|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was noted as "Haguro Shrine in Koji Village, enshrining Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto."Historical board at Haguro Shrine. It was founded during the {{ill|Bunmei|en|Bunmei|qid=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei era|1=Bunmei}} (1469–1487) after deities were transferred from {{ill|Dewa Shrine|en|Dewa Shrine|qid=Q11395802|lt=Dewa Shrine|1=Dewa Shrine}} at {{ill|Mount Haguro|en|Mount Haguro|qid=Q3090765|lt=Mount Haguro (Yamagata Prefecture)|1=Mount Haguro}} in the {{ill|Dewa Province|en|Dewa Province|qid=Q876032|lt=Dewa Province|1=Dewa Province}}. The current location, previously known as Mount Haguro, served as the shrine's site. In {{ill|Genroku|en|Genroku|qid=Q1325001|lt=Genroku|1=Genroku}} 16 (1703), the shrine buildings were restored, and the shrine was designated the guardian deity of Koji Village. The stone shrine buildings were constructed in {{ill|Enkyō (Edo period)|en|Enkyō (Edo period)|qid=Q1183728|lt=Enkyō|1=Enkyō (Edo period)}} 5 (1748). Due to the remote location of Mount Haguro, the shrine was relocated in {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|qid=Q1325317|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka}} 4 (1847) to its current site, incorporating a local {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine was then renamed Haguro Shrine. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872) - Designated as a Village Shrine. * {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō era|1=Taishō era}} 10 (1921) - Recognized as a {{ill|Shinzen-heihakuryō Shrine|ja|近代社格制度#神饌幣帛料供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzen-heihakuryō Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei era|1=Heisei era}} 14 (2002) - Certified as a "Model Shrine for Promotion and Education" by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|qid=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}} for the period from 2002 to 2007{{Cite web|url=https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/6686 |title=Haguro Shrine |publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines |accessdate=2022-01-02}}. == festivals == === Spring Festival === Known as "Shunki-sai" (春季祭) or Spring Festival, this event features {{ill|Miko-mai|ja|巫女舞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miko-mai|1=miko mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479841}} dances offered at the shrine. === Nagoshi-no-Ōharae (Summer Purification Ritual) === This event, held in summer, purifies the sins and impurities of the first half of the year. Attendees pass through a {{ill|Chinowa|ja|茅の輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinowa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大祓|1=Ōharae-shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11261910}} to pray for good health and safety for the latter half of the year. This ritual was revived in {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei era|1=Heisei era}} 20 (2008){{Cite web|url=https://hagurojinja.amebaownd.com/posts/5010918 |title=Summer Purification Ritual |publisher=Haguro Shrine |accessdate=2022-01-02}}. ==== Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the Grass Ring) ==== This nationwide Shinto ritual involves passing through a large grass ring to purify one's body and mind, ensuring health and safety{{Cite web|url=https://precious.jp/articles/-/19762 |title=Chinowa Kuguri: History and Meaning |publisher=Precious |accessdate=2022-01-03}}. ==== Onnokiba (Demon's Fang) ==== A local Suzu custom where hardened rice cakes from New Year are broken and eaten on July 1. The act symbolizes driving away internal evil spirits{{Cite web|url=https://www.hokurikushinkansen-navi.jp/pc/news/article.php?id=NEWS0000024371 |title=Onnokiba Tradition Revived |publisher=[[Hokkoku Shimbun]] |accessdate=2022-01-02}}. === Autumn Festival === This grand festival is the most significant event of the year. During {{ill|Kōka|en|Kōka|qid=Q1325317|lt=Kōka|1=Kōka}} 4 (1847), the shrine relocation from Mount Haguro to its current site was commemorated with a lion dance procession. After the {{ill|Taisei Hōkan|ja|大政奉還|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisei Hōkan|qq=}}, the festival date was fixed to September 19. == Annual Events == * New Year's Festival (January 1) * {{ill|Sagichō Fire Festival|en|Sagichō Fire Festival|qid=Q9332740|lt=Sagichō|1=Sagichō Fire Festival}} (January 15) * Foundation Day Celebration (February 11) * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}} (March 19) * Spring Festival (April 19) * Nagoshi-no-Ōharae (June 30) * Autumn Festival (September 19) * Kankōsai (September 20) * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November 19) * Year-End Purification (December 31) * Monthly Worship (1st of every month) * Monthly Festival (19th of every month) == location and Access == '''Address:''' * {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} {{ill|Suzu, Ishikawa|en|Suzu, Ishikawa|qid=Q846345|lt=Suzu|1=Suzu, Ishikawa}} Shoin-machi Koji 16-102 '''Transportation:''' * City Bus (Kinoura Line) - Get off at Shoin, 7 minutes on foot. == External Links == * [https://hagurojinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Website of Haguro Shrine] * [https://ameblo.jp/hagurotetsu/ Blog of the Shrine Priest] * [https://www.ishikawa-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/j1836/ Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Association] * [https://www.jinjahoncho.or.jp/6686 Association of Shinto Shrines] == References == {{Reflist}} }} {{translated page|ja|羽黒神社 (珠洲市)|version=98250408|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| {{Commons category|Haguro-jinja (Suzu)}} [[ja:羽黒神社 (珠洲市)]] {{wikidata link|Q126020334}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suzu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:History of Suzu, Ishikawa]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
